mapper.select: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM coupon_issue ci LEFT JOIN coupon c ON ci.couponNo = c.couponNo

SELECT ci.issueNo AS issueNo, ci.couponNo AS couponNo, ci.memberUid AS memberUid, ci.status AS status, ci.createdAt AS createdAt, ci.updatedAt AS updatedAt, c.couponType AS couponType, c.sellerUid AS sellerUid, c.benefit AS benefit, c.name AS couponName, c.startDate AS startDate, c.expireDate AS expireDate, c.notice AS notice, s.companyName AS companyName, s.status AS sellerStatus, m.status AS memberStatus FROM coupon_issue ci LEFT JOIN coupon c ON ci.couponNo = c.couponNo LEFT JOIN seller s ON c.sellerUid = s.uid LEFT JOIN member m ON ci.memberUid = m.uid
ORDER BY ci.issueNo DESC LIMIT #{offset}, #{pageSize}

SELECT ci.issueNo, ci.couponNo, ci.memberUid, ci.status, ci.createdAt, ci.updatedAt, c.couponType, c.sellerUid, c.benefit, c.name AS couponName, c.startDate, c.expireDate, c.notice, s.companyName AS companyName FROM coupon_issue ci LEFT JOIN coupon c ON ci.couponNo = c.couponNo LEFT JOIN seller s ON c.sellerUid = s.uid WHERE ci.issueNo = #{issueNo}
mapper.update: UPDATE coupon_issue SET status = 3 WHERE couponNo = #{couponNo} AND status = 0
UPDATE coupon_issue ci JOIN coupon c ON ci.couponNo = c.couponNo SET ci.status = 2 WHERE ci.status = 0 AND c.expireDate < CURDATE()
